column1: # Alpha-globin deletion/triplication alleles — copy-number signatures
# GENERATED by bin/make_hba_deletion_alleles.py. Do not hand-edit.
# THESE ARE NOT BREAKPOINTS. Alpha-cluster NAHR breakpoints sit inside
# near-identical homology boxes and are published against varying builds;
# hand-typing GRCh38 coordinates for them would be guesswork. Alleles are
# therefore defined by WHICH SEGMENTS THEY REMOVE (see hba_segments.bed),
# which is what a depth caller measures anyway.
# approx_size is DOCUMENTARY ONLY. Never use it as a coordinate.
# basis:  observed   = measured in this project's data, cited in note
#         literature = allele composition is textbook; SIZE is approximate
# d_* columns are the copy change on the AFFECTED chromosome:
#   0 intact · -1 lost · h disrupted/hybrid (partial depth signal) · + gained
# depth_distinguishable=no:A|B means depth ALONE cannot separate those
# alleles. The caller MUST report the group, not arbitrarily pick one.
# Resolving within a group needs the deletion extent or a junction read.
allele
--SEA
column11: OBSERVED in THAL1: normalized depth 0.45-0.60 across chr16:164000-186000, HBZ 1.00, flanks 1.05. Spares HBZ. Commonest 2-gene allele in SE Asia.

column1: --MED
column11: Removes both alpha genes, spares HBZ. Depth-degenerate with --SEA; separating them needs the extent or a junction read.

column1: --FIL
column11: Larger; extends into HBZ. Loss of HBZ signal is the discriminator vs --SEA/--MED.

column1: --THAI
column11: As --FIL, also removes HBZ. Depth-degenerate with --FIL.

Rightward NAHR between Z boxes; fuses HBA2 5' to HBA1 3' into ONE hybrid gene. Neither gene body vanishes cleanly - the deleted 3.7 kb lies between them, so INTER_A2_A1 is the diagnostic segment, not HBA1/HBA2. Most common alpha-thal deletion worldwide.
Leftward NAHR between X boxes; removes HBA2, leaves HBA1 intact.

column1: anti-3.7
column11: Reciprocal product of the -a3.7 NAHR: alpha-gene TRIPLICATION (aaa/). Gains a gene rather than losing one. Benign alone, but modifies beta-thal severity, and a caller that only looks for losses will silently miss it.
